mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or the signal Sharpe ratio remains below 0.5 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and rising housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, driving sustained equity appreciation over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative average 60-day forward return, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a deceleration in the index of industrial production signals capacity constraints and margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal generates positive forward returns in late-cycle regimes, or the predictive power vanishes when regime filters are applied (IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects strong economic momentum that translates into immediate corporate earnings optimism, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Walk-forward testing shows no significant correlation between GDPC1 growth rates and 5-day forward returns (Spearman rho < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds a 3.5% year-over-year growth rate, anticipated aggressive monetary tightening compresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally following CPI spikes above 3.5%, or the 60-day forward return distribution shows a positive mean with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and a falling unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict returns, with a combined Rank IC below 0.03, or the interaction effect reverses direction during the tested period.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery environment, a sharp rebound in housing starts signals renewed consumer confidence and credit availability, generating immediate positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Housing start rebounds produce negative or flat 5-day forward returns in recovery regimes, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.
